finding: E8 golden BOTH surfaces: ui+desktop types.ts BYTE-IDENTICAL and ui+desktop openapi.json canonically-equal (jq -S) vs .extraction/baseline; regen then restored via git checkout. Pure infra move — no wire surface moved.

SSRF guard moved verbatim: is_blocked_v4/v6 (RFC1918/loopback/link-local/CGNAT/ULA/IPv4-mapped), validate_outbound_url credential+scheme+DNS check, GuardingResolver connect-time re-resolution, and per-hop redirect_blocked_reason are byte-for-byte unchanged. All 25 in-source SSRF tests moved intact. No policy constant altered.

At-rest crypto moved verbatim except AppError source + storage_key path: encrypt_secret/decrypt_secret still call pgp_sym_encrypt/decrypt via runtime query_as, MIN_STORAGE_KEY_LENGTH=32 refusal unchanged, SecretView still serializes '<redacted>'. resolve_optional_secret's fail-closed-to-plaintext-column semantics preserved (nested if-let identical to the let-chain). No secret leak surface changed.

OnceCell<Option<String>> storage-key global moved verbatim (let-chain desugared). One static per process regardless of owning crate; init_storage_key (boot, once) writes it via the crate::core::secrets shim, storage_key() reads observe the same value. Idempotent first-call-wins + <32-char warn behaviour unchanged.

grep confirms ZERO compile-time query!/query_as!/query_scalar! in secret.rs — it uses runtime sqlx::query_as('SELECT pgp_sym_encrypt($1,$2)'), which is checked at run time not build time. ziee-framework stays build-DB-free; cd sdk && cargo check --workspace needs no build DB (exit 0).

core::outbound retargets ziee_framework::url_validator::{OutboundUrlPolicy,build_validated_client,validate_outbound_url} (was crate::utils::url_validator) — completes BG Decision 5. auth::providers::{oauth2,apple} keep naming crate::core::outbound (12 sites unchanged). Same 3 functions.

Shim `pub use ziee_framework::url_validator::*;` re-exports the full public surface (OutboundUrlPolicy + constants STRICT/PUBLIC_HTTP_OR_HTTPS/MCP_USER/DEV_LOCAL, OutboundUrlError, validate_outbound_url, build_validated_client, validated_client_builder). All 16 crate::utils::url_validator::* consumers resolve unchanged.

Shim `pub use ziee_framework::secret::*;` re-exports encrypt_secret/decrypt_secret/resolve_optional_secret/SecretView/mask_secret. All 16 crate::common::secret::* consumers (llm_provider x3, mcp x2, web_search, lit_search, llm_repository, llm_local_runtime, auth, ...) resolve unchanged.

Shim `pub use ziee_framework::secrets::*;` re-exports init_storage_key + storage_key. lib.rs:38 `pub use core::secrets::{init_storage_key, storage_key}` + main.rs boot call + 8 module repos + core/events.rs all resolve unchanged.

SecretView<T> has NO JsonSchema impl (documented at module top) — it never enters any schemars-derived DTO, so moving the type's short ident across the crate boundary cannot move an OpenAPI schema name or $ref. This is why the E8 golden stays byte-identical. Confirmed: no `impl JsonSchema for SecretView` anywhere.

AppError repointed from crate::common::AppError to ziee_core::AppError — same underlying type (crate::common::AppError was a re-export of ziee_core::AppError). AppError::internal_error/database_error signatures + messages unchanged; the '<32 chars' and database_error mapping produce identical errors.

reqwest declared default-features=false + [rustls-tls,charset,http2] byte-matching the ziee server base decl, so cargo feature-unification adds nothing new to the server build (no default features flipped on). tokio gains rt (spawn_blocking in GuardingResolver) + macros (#[tokio::test]s). url/thiserror/once_cell versions match the server catalog → single Cargo.lock unifies without dup builds. cargo check -p ziee exit 0.

let-chain -> nested if-let desugar (TRANSFORMS D3) is a pure syntactic downgrade for the 2021 SDK workspace: same short-circuit order (encrypted.is_some THEN storage_key().is_some), same decrypt/fallback branches, same tracing::error on failure. Control flow identical; framework unit tests pass under 2021.

Desktop surface golden also byte-identical (types.ts) + canonical (openapi.json) vs baseline — neither url_validator, secret, nor secrets contributes any aide/schemars-registered handler or DTO to either binary's spec.

Scope held tight: 3 new framework files + 3 modified framework files (lib/Cargo/lock) + 4 modified ziee files (3 shims + outbound) + Cargo.lock. No consumer edited (shims preserve every path); no domain type moved. Well under any scope guard.
